title: Struve Geodetic Arc - Wikipedia
content:
| Attribute | Detail |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Exact Length (Historical)** | Over 2,820 kilometres (1,750 mi) |
| **Exact Length (Modern)** | 2,821.853 ± 0.012 kilometres |
| **Northern Endpoint** | Fuglenes in Hammerfest, Norway (70°40′12″N 23°39′48″E / 70° 40' 11.23″ N) |
| **Southern Endpoint** | Staro-Nekrasivka (Staro-Nekrassowka) near Ismail/Black Sea, Ukraine (45°19′57.5″N 28°55′40″E / 45° 20' 02.94″ N) |

| Survey Component | Quantity |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Main Triangles** | 258 |
| **Geodetic Vertices** | 265 |
| **Main Station Points** | 265 |
| **World Heritage Protected Points** | 34 (Commemorative plaques or built obelisks) |

### Dates of Measurement
*   **1816–1855:** Years the chain was established and used by Friedrich Georg Wilhelm von Struve.
*   **1816–1852:** Specific period cited for the measurements of the 30° Meridian Arc.
*   **1740:** Date of an earlier survey mentioned for comparison.
*   **2005:** Year the measurement was repeated using modern satellite navigation.
*   **19th Century to beginning of 20th Century:** Description of geodesic and map-making works in the Balkans by the Russian Czarist Army.

### Measurement Method
*   **Historical Method:** Survey triangulations (triangulation chain). 
*   **Modern Method:** Satellite navigation.
*   **Raw Angle Data vs. Computed Distances:** The specific distinction between raw angle data and computed distances is not explicitly stated in the text.
